title: DeviceAuthPollResponse
description:
`POST /v1/login/token` response ‚Äî the poll `status`; a `granted` poll carries the SESSION's
workspace-scoped bearer credential (the promoted flow code ‚Äî returned here and stored from this
ONE field), the session id, and the joined workspace. A re-poll of an approved flow returns the
same answer.

credential.description:
The session's plaintext bearer credential ‚Äî present ONLY when `status` is `granted`.
Returned once per poll; the server stores only its sha256.

device_id.description:
The RETIRED device wire's grant id ‚Äî parse-only fallback for a producer predating
[`Self::session_id`]; never served by the session wire.

hint.description:
The invitation's first-destination hint ‚Äî present only on a `granted` poll whose flow
carried an invite token naming one.

link_status.description:
The RETIRED device-link spelling of [`Self::session_status`] ‚Äî parse-only fallback for a
producer predating the session wire. Absent ‚áí treat as `"active"`.

session_id.description:
The minted SESSION's id ‚Äî the session-model login wire's grant half (a session = user √ó
workspace √ó installation; the credential is workspace-scoped). Present ONLY when `status`
is `granted` on a session-serving producer. **Additive.**

session_status.description:
The session's born status ‚Äî `"active"`, or `"pending"` while the workspace's
session-approval knob holds it. Absent ‚áí treat as active. **Additive.**

workspace.description: The joined workspace ‚Äî present ONLY when `status` is `granted`.

DeviceAuthHint.description:
The first-destination HINT an accepted invitation named ‚Äî decorated onto a `granted` poll (and
the direct-accept answer) so the logged-in client's first `add` can target it. `kind` is the
bundle catalog's own tag (`skill` today) or the literal `channel` ‚Äî displayed and routed on,
never trusted as authority.

kind.description: What the hinted thing is: the catalog's `kind` tag, or `channel`.

name.description: The hinted bundle's or channel's name in the joined workspace.

DeviceAuthPollStatus.description:
A login-flow poll status (snake_case). `granted` carries the credential + the joined
workspace; every other status carries only itself.

oneOf.description: Not yet approved ‚Äî keep polling at the interval.
The flow was denied at the approval page.

DeviceAuthWorkspace.description:
The workspace context a `granted` poll carries ‚Äî everything the CLI needs to record what it
logged into (the id it scopes requests by, the address slug it joined at, and a display name).

name.description: The workspace's ADDRESS slug (what the human typed at `login`).

workspace_id.description: The workspace id (the `{ws}` path segment of every subsequent request).
